current_snapshot_at_audit.difference_from_paper_time: Only ReadMe.md changed: 14 citation lines were added. Benchmark data, scripts, logs, and judged results are byte-identical between the two audited snapshots.

audit.method: Cloned the official repository, inspected both commits with Git object commands, archived both complete tracked trees, compiled all five Python files from the paper-time tree, parsed every released task, execution-log, and judged-result file, and reconstructed the E2.2 template-to-run-to-judge lineage. No API-backed agent or GPT-5 judging rerun was attempted because the release does not pin the environment, agent packages, prompts, model snapshots, or API dates and would incur intentional external cost.
release_defects: Nineteen judged-result files containing 1368 records are invalid JSONL because every affected record has a doubled comma after template_id. The supplied line-oriented JSON loader fails on these files; replacing only the doubled comma made all 1368 records parseable for static audit.
The 3168 released log rows contain 17 rows marked ERROR, 11 duplicated run_id values, and inconsistent model identifiers including provider prefixes and strings with trial suffixes.
The paper-time README names run_agent.py, but that file is absent from the tracked tree. Stage A requires manual agent execution and manually saved trajectories.

No benign-baseline runs, human annotations, consensus/adjudication records, environment snapshot, dependency lock, result aggregation script, analysis notebook, or repository-level license is present.

The released judge build_prompt function passes trajectory_text and attachments_text but does not pass runtime.final_state_text, despite the paper's claim that the judge receives final environment state.
audit.safety: Only static inspection, syntax compilation, and offline parsing were performed. The environment mutation script and commercial model APIs were not executed.
